main_ingredients: high fructose corn syrup or sugar

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of Sprite is approximately 355 mL (355 g), providing about 140 kcal and 38 g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, SPRITE, lemon-lime, without caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174852)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry FDC ID 174852 is an exact match for Sprite lemon-lime carbonated soft drink. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 mL) of beverage.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for Sprite is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the peer-reviewed International Tables of GI (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008; Foster-Powell et al., AJCN 2002). Sprite lemon-lime soda is listed with GI ~63 (glucose reference = 100). Glycemic load per 355 mL serving calculated as: (63 × 38 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.9, rounded to 24.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (7 mg per 100 mL)
hypertension.guidance: Sprite contains only 7 mg sodium per 100 mL, posing minimal direct sodium burden for hypertensive individuals; however, its high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic risk, which are secondary drivers of elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.6 g per 100 mL) and medium glycemic index (GI 63)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sprite delivers 10.6 g of free sugars per 100 mL with a GI of 63, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and prefer water or unsweetened beverages.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to hypertriglyceridaemia and visceral adiposity
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Sprite is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral fat, and higher cardiovascular risk; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise intake in line with WHO free sugar guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sprite is a globally distributed lemon-lime flavoured carbonated soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company. It is a clear, colourless beverage with a crisp, sweet-tart citrus flavour profile derived from citric acid and natural lemon-lime flavourings. Sprite contains no caffeine and no artificial colours. Per 100 mL, it provides approximately 40 kcal, 10.6 g of carbohydrate (entirely as free sugars), 0 g protein, and 0 g fat, with a low sodium content of 7 mg. The glycemic index of Sprite is approximately 63 (medium GI), and a standard 355 mL can delivers a glycemic load of approximately 24, making it a significant source of rapidly absorbed glucose. Clinically, Sprite is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free sugar content and medium GI, and for those managing cardiovascular risk due to the association between sugar-sweetened beverage consumption and hypertriglyceridaemia. In Singapore, Sprite is widely available at fast food outlets, Japanese restaurant chains such as Sukiya, convenience stores, and vending machines. It is commonly consumed as a meal accompaniment or standalone refreshment. Nutritionally, Sprite provides no meaningful micronutrients and should be considered an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary component.
common_user_queries: What is the sugar content of Sprite?
What is the GI of Sprite?
How much sodium is in Sprite?
Can I drink Sprite if I have high blood pressure?
What are the ingredients in Sprite?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174852), an exact match for Sprite lemon-lime carbonated soft drink. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed International Tables of GI. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from the approved primary source.
notes: Sprite is a commercially manufactured carbonated soft drink. protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174852. fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174852. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, dietary_fibre_g, vitamin_c_mg, and vitamin_a_mcg_rae are all confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this product. The cuisine tag 'Japanese' in the input reflects the restaurant context (Sukiya, a Japanese beef bowl chain) rather than the origin of Sprite itself; cuisine has been corrected to 'Global / Commercial Beverage'. The glycemic_index is set to 63 per University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is medium. Glycemic load per 355 mL serving = (63 × 38) / 100 = 23.94, rounded to 24.0. Japanese local name スプライト (Supuraito) included as Sprite is sold in Japan and the restaurant context is Japanese. Chinese name 雪碧 (Xuě bì) is the official Simplified and Traditional Chinese brand name used across Greater China and Singapore.
